question: Is the courtroom environment (space and furniture) rendered in a photo-realistic style with warm overhead lighting?
Is the bailiff/security guard in the foreground rendered in a photo-realistic style (realistic skin and fabric)?
Is the judge rendered in a woodcut print style, featuring coarse line engravings and strong black-white contrast?
Is the lawyer rendered in a hard black ink line comic style with strong contrast and bold outlines?
Do the lawyer's hands exhibit exaggerated perspective, consistent with the specified comic style?
Is the defendant rendered in a stop-motion claymation style, exhibiting clay-like textures, wrinkles, or molded forms?
Is the witness rendered in a classical oil painting style, featuring soft gradients and visible canvas texture?
Does the display screen show a Mondrian-style (De Stijl) geometric composition with red, yellow, and blue rectangles and black lines?
Is the Mondrian-style content on the screen visually distinct (picture-in-picture) from the surrounding photorealistic environment?
Are distinct style boundaries maintained between the characters (no unintended blending/bleeding of styles)?
Is there unified lighting (warm overhead) and perspective across all differently-styled elements in the scene?
Is the judge's gavel-striking action depicted as frozen mid-air?
Is the lawyer reaching to hand a document toward the witness stand?
Is the defendant turning back to look and partially occluded by the bailiff?
Are all six specified subjects (bailiff, judge, lawyer, defendant, witness, display screen) present in the image?
Is the image completely free of readable text (including on the display screen and documents)?
